what_it_does: Defines layout primitives and a z-index layering scale: a :root z-index token ladder (base through max), plus Every-Layout-style composable classes — .stack, .cluster, .sidebar, .center, .cover, .reel, .frame-ratio, .grid-fit, and .switcher — each responsive by construction and tunable via per-instance custom properties.
observations: Z-index ladder: base 0, raised 10, sticky 100, nav 200, overlay 300, modal 400, popover 500, toast 600, command 700, max 9999
Header comment names the z-scale 'the only legal stacking order'
.stack — vertical flex with --gap (default --d-4), zeroed margin-block on children, min-height 0
.sidebar — flex-wrap rail+content that collapses to a stack with no media query (rail --side 280px, content wrap threshold --content-min 60%)
.center — measure-capped centered column with content-box and --gutter padding
.cover — vertically centered region via margin-block:auto on .cover-main
.reel — single-row horizontal scroller with scroll-snap x proximity
.switcher — quantity-query: items in a row until --threshold (460px), then all snap to a column together via flex-basis:calc((threshold - 100%) * 999)
standouts: The .switcher flex-basis calc trick that flips the whole set to a column at once (a CSS quantity query, no media query)
The z-index scale declared as the single legal stacking order with named semantic tiers (overlay/modal/popover/toast/command)
conventions: Explicitly credits Every-Layout as the source of the primitive set
Per-instance tuning via custom properties documented per class
@kind other annotations on the z-index integer tokens
References spacing/measure tokens rather than raw values
notes: The single home for responsive layout primitives (Every-Layout style) plus the semantic z-index ladder; compose these classes instead of writing bespoke flex/grid per component.
questions: Where are the spacing tokens (--d-2/--d-3/--d-4, --space-fluid-md) and --measure defined? (--measure and --space-fluid-md appear in sizing.css; --d-* are referenced but not defined there)
What defines the --d-* spacing scale that several primitives default to?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header promises composable, media-query-free responsive primitives plus a z-index scale, and the file delivers exactly that set.
intention_signals: Trying to make 'responsive & computed' automatic by giving components a small set of intrinsic-responsive primitives to compose, eliminating ad-hoc flex/grid and z-index conflicts.
novelty: The container-driven .switcher and .sidebar collapse behaviour achieved with flex-basis math rather than breakpoints, and the named command/toast z-tiers.
contribution: Contributes the Every-Layout primitive set (stack/cluster/sidebar/center/cover/reel/frame/grid-fit/switcher) and the semantic z-index ladder as composable, single-source building blocks.
summary_for_embedding: ConceptV design-system CSS defining a semantic z-index scale and Every-Layout-style composable, media-query-free responsive layout primitives (stack, cluster, sidebar, center, cover, reel, grid-fit, switcher).
intention_for_embedding: To make layout responsive-by-construction through a small set of composable intrinsic primitives and a single legal z-index stacking order.
